title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
summary: Jonathan Ross discusses hardware optimization for LLM inference, the economic principles driving compute demand, and skills needed in the AI era. The conversation covers Groq's LPU architecture versus GPUs, the productivity paradox in AI, and how cheaper inference will fuel additional demand across applications.
writeup: Julia Turc interviews Groq founder Jonathan Ross on hardware optimization for LLM inference, centered on Groq's LPU architecture and how it differs from GPUs. The discussion connects this to the economics of compute: Jevons Paradox implies that cheaper inference drives additional demand rather than reducing it, fueling more applications across the field. Ross also addresses the productivity paradox in AI and the skills relevant in the current era, with concepts spanning test-time compute, scheduling, and Mixture of Experts.
